110839.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of referenda. The municipal council is elected to make difficult trade-offs. It is important to involve residents in other ways, for example through consultation and participation. And of course through council members rooted in their village.

110843.explanation: We welcome all initiatives from residents who unite, including to generate energy. Where necessary, the municipality can assist with the establishment. A financial contribution from the municipality is a possibility, but does not always apply.

110844.explanation: Our location is interesting for the logistics sector and provides employment. We critically review new companies that are heavily dependent on labor migrants, weighing the consequences for facilities and the community heavily. But we do not exclude any specific sectors.

110847.explanation: Employers certainly have a responsibility in housing seasonal workers. By the way, the percentage of labor migrants working in agriculture and horticulture within our municipality is small.

110855.explanation: We want to allow this to a small extent, for example with retiring farmers. Provided it fits the surroundings and neighboring farmers are not affected in their operations. We also support care accommodation on private property. We do not want to allow large-scale projects in rural areas.

110856.explanation: Everyone should be able to play sports, especially children! There are already options to request support for sports club membership fees. We want to maintain that.

110858.explanation: This requires a good multi-year maintenance plan and a schedule for replacing school buildings that have reached the end of their lifespan.

110860.explanation: The municipality already contributes a lot to sports clubs, especially in facilities and fields. We want to maintain this. The association life is important to us.

110862.explanation: The CDA wants to increase safety in our municipality. Community enforcement officers contribute to that. Rules only make sense if we can enforce them. For that, we need these officers.

110863.explanation: Camera surveillance makes the environment safer and ensures we can address the perpetrators of nuisance. A good idea!

110864.explanation: For the CDA, it is important that victims can access a women's shelter. Good support comes first! If needed, we support establishing one in our municipality. But given our municipality's scale, joining the initiative from Alphen or Gouda is more logical.

110865.explanation: The existing starter loan is well used, and sometimes provides just the push starters need to buy a house. But above all, we want to focus on realizing more affordable homes!

110868.explanation: We are not in favor of mass overcrowding of holiday homes. But customization is paramount here.

110870.explanation: Providing a counterpart is, to us, part of taking responsibility for the local community. For us, this can also be volunteer work. That is just as valuable as paid work.

110875.explanation: Fortunately, this is already the case in many places. Especially around schools and shopping areas, this is important. The CDA stands for bicycle safety. Lowering the speed limit in built-up areas helps with this.

110876.explanation: In principle, this should be possible, but it is not the case that the municipality must pay for this in all cases.

110877.explanation: We want the quality of youth care to be good. Not at any cost, but the price should not be the leading factor.

110878.explanation: The property tax (OZB) should not rise further than inflation. Given the municipality’s financial situation, however, lowering the OZB is a promise that no party can keep.

110880.explanation: Tourists make use of our amenities. The costs to maintain those amenities have increased, so an increase in this tax is reasonable.

110881.explanation: In recent years, several business locations have disappeared to make room for housing. Employment is also important, so we must compensate for this. We favor more space for businesses, preferably by expanding existing business parks.

110882.explanation: In our municipality, we have found a good balance between peace, family, sports, and commerce on Sundays, and we want to maintain that. Supermarkets are already allowed to open every Sunday.

110883.explanation: Just like various other groups, people with a residence permit can already be given priority. They are therefore one of many groups.

110884.explanation: Most waste can already be delivered free of charge at the recycling center. This also prevents illegal dumping.

110885.explanation: We cherish our beautiful rural areas and focus on solar panels on roofs and along infrastructure. Our agricultural land is too scarce and valuable to use for this purpose.

110888.explanation: A coffee shop contributes to normalizing drug use. That is contrary to the healthy society we collectively strive for. Moreover, coffee shops attract crime.

110891.explanation: Room rentals currently mainly take place in the center of Bodegraven, often to labor migrants. This leads to undesirable, unsafe situations for both residents and the surroundings. We are open to smart solutions for the housing shortage, such as dividing homes. But that must be appropriate.

110892.explanation: There is already a shortage of parking spaces in the center of Bodegraven. Removing more parking spaces would only worsen this problem.

110893.explanation: However, we are considering measures to better redirect through traffic around the center.

110895.explanation: The CDA supports the Bodegraven loop. But the costs for a connection between a provincial road (N11) and a national highway (A12) should not fall on our municipality. We want to invest in the safety of the many cyclists who have to cross there daily. Address that crossing!

110896.explanation: These people deserve good housing. Employers have an important responsibility to contribute to this. We support the smallest scale possible housing. But the municipality may not prohibit building a location solely because it is for labor migrants.

110839.explanation: It is important that residents can express their opinions so that the silent majority is also heard. However, we doubt whether costly referendums are the best means for this.

110843.explanation: Several initiatives have already started without municipal support, so it seems unnecessary. We prefer to translate support for our residents into lower property tax.

110844.explanation: We value locally rooted businesses that are at the heart of the community. Given the limited space on business parks, large distribution centers do not seem a logical use to us.

110847.explanation: Providing housing can, in our view, be a good part of good employer conduct, and it also prevents mass housing of seasonal workers elsewhere. However, the housing must be of good quality and meet reasonable conditions, such as notice periods.

110855.explanation: Customization on a small scale should be possible, but our rural area is not designed to add large numbers of homes.

110856.explanation: This is already reimbursed via the Green Heart Pass; in our view, additional municipal support is therefore unnecessary.

110858.explanation: Several schools are outdated and due for major maintenance. Funds have been reserved for this in the coming years.

110860.explanation: The municipality already does quite a lot in sports; basically, every club offering a sport in demand should be able to support itself.

110862.explanation: Safety on the streets is a major concern of our residents. Blue presence on the streets seems a good option, provided community enforcement officers have the powers to act decisively.

110863.explanation: Partly on the initiative of the VVD, a pilot with camera surveillance in Bodegraven has finally started recently. The first results are positive, entrepreneurs are satisfied, and the number of incidents is decreasing. We would like to see this preventive surveillance in more places.

110864.explanation: We organize care tasks regionally; in our view, it is not necessary to have everything available within our municipality itself, as long as it is nearby.

110865.explanation: The municipality currently has a starter loan scheme that is well used. We would like to continue this to give young people in our municipality a chance to take their first step on the housing market.

110868.explanation: Many holiday homes in our municipality are located in vulnerable areas with narrow roads. Permanent residence there leads to more traffic and unsafe situations. We do not think that is wise.

110870.explanation: Benefits are an important safety net, but we must certainly not turn them into a hammock. We are generous towards those who cannot work. But from everyone else, an effort may be expected; there is plenty of (volunteer) work to do!

110875.explanation: Almost all built-up areas already have a 30 km/h limit, but the police enforce it little. Moreover, the speed limit doesn't tell the whole story. For us, it is mainly important that roads are clear and safe, and that through traffic uses ring roads as much as possible. That helps more than a '30' sign.

110876.explanation: As far as care is offered by the municipality, we see no reason to hire additional providers for specific needs.

110877.explanation: What matters more is that the results are good. A cheap provider who does not solve the problems well ends up costing us more.

110878.explanation: Our municipality has a high property tax (ozb), which was also necessary to get the finances in order. Now that we are on the right track, it seems healthy to us to look at where taxes can be lowered.

110880.explanation: As far as we are concerned, the tourist tax could be a bit higher, but on the condition that the revenues are invested in public spaces. This way, it won't become a hidden cash cow, it contributes to our appeal, and our residents benefit as well.

110881.explanation: Our business community has been lacking space for a long time. Both due to growth and the conversion of outdated industrial areas into housing. So it is important that good and sustainable new sites are created for local entrepreneurs.

110882.explanation: The Sunday shopping in our municipality works well for supermarkets and other entrepreneurs, but other shops are often quiet on Sundays. As far as we are concerned, there is no longer a need for the municipality to decide when shops may open: make it free, and it will probably stay the same.

110883.explanation: This priority is experienced as unfair by many residents, especially by starters who have been on the waiting list for years.

110884.explanation: There is already a lot that is free. We find it fair that certain waste streams must be paid for separately when they are costly to process.

110885.explanation: It was agreed in the past that the roofs of business premises must be fully covered first, before looking at solar panels in meadows or wind turbines. We want to respect that agreement.

110888.explanation: It seems interesting to us to see whether a coffeeshop can help combat nuisance, since currently dangerous rides under the influence come from surrounding cities. But as long as there is no good evidence that it helps, we are against the establishment of coffeeshops.

110891.explanation: Renting a room can be a fine form of housing for young people moving out for the first time. But quality standards must be met.

110892.explanation: On the contrary! By creating more parking spaces or larger garages, parking problems can be solved, and space can be made for new homes above the shops.

110893.explanation: Shopkeepers indicate that accessibility of the center is essential for vitality. The prolonged closure of the center bridge has cost entrepreneurs tens of thousands of euros. We do not want a car-free ghost town without shops.

110895.explanation: Most of the costs must be borne by the national and provincial governments, but we find it justifiable to contribute ourselves if that speeds up realization. After all, the Bodegraven loop solves many local problems.

110896.explanation: Recently, plans were approved for a small-scale location with 100 beds. If this proves successful, we are open to more locations for decent housing. We do not see large-scale housing as desirable.

110839.explanation: Our democracy is based on representatives elected every four years. Referendums undermine good consultation; it is better to create sufficient support beforehand.

110843.explanation: Bodegraven-Reeuwijk supports energy cooperatives that generate profitable energy for their members; extra financial support is not needed.

110844.explanation: The municipality has limited space for business parks, which is best used for high-quality companies. Large-scale distribution centers fit better in surrounding municipalities.

110847.explanation: Labor migrants must be properly housed, independent of their employer, to prevent exploitation.

110855.explanation: More interests play a role than just those of farmers. Burgerbelangen is basically positive towards farmers who have space for a single dwelling. At the same time, other factors must be considered, such as traffic safety, preservation of pastures, the living environment, and neighbors.

110856.explanation: Everyone must have the opportunity to be a member of at least one sports club. When financial barriers prevent this, the municipality should provide support to enable participation.

110858.explanation: School buildings are renewed and improved according to an established housing plan. The current state of the buildings is good, and where necessary they will be addressed in the coming years.

110860.explanation: Sports clubs are very important for our villages. If they are vital, with sufficient active members, the municipality can financially support them in difficult periods.

110862.explanation: More visible police on the street helps to prevent and reduce nuisance. Support by enforcement officers (boa’s) can be useful, provided they have sufficient powers. Expansion of the number of enforcers is only desirable within that context.

110863.explanation: In some places in our municipality there is already camera surveillance, which has proven effective. However, Burgerbelangen first looks at other solutions to prevent nuisance and insecurity before considering expanding camera surveillance.

110864.explanation: It is still unclear whether there is a necessity, but if there is, there should also be a women's shelter ('stay-away-from-my-husband' house) in Bodegraven-Reeuwijk.

110865.explanation: The municipality has a well-functioning starter loan scheme. This scheme must be maintained to support beginning buyers.

110868.explanation: Converting a holiday home into a permanent residence is drastic and irreversible. Every application must be carefully assessed per home.

110870.explanation: It is not appropriate to ask everyone for a counter-performance. Where possible, socially meaningful work can be requested, but never as a condition for a social assistance benefit.

110875.explanation: Many roads in built-up areas have a 50 km/h limit, which is too high. 30 km/h everywhere significantly improves traffic safety.

110876.explanation: It is desirable, but the most important thing remains that someone receives quality care, regardless of faith, culture, or beliefs.

110877.explanation: When selecting youth care organizations, the focus should be on effectiveness and quality, not primarily on price.

110878.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is high. A reduction is difficult in the short term, but should be the goal.

110880.explanation: Due to the large number of tourists, the tourist tax is an important means to receive financial compensation.

110881.explanation: Entrepreneurs request expansion of industrial estates. Rijnhoek 2 and Zoutman are suitable locations.

110882.explanation: There is little demand for Sunday shopping, but entrepreneurs should have the choice to keep them.

110883.explanation: The municipality follows the national government's task assignment. Once that is fulfilled, local residents have priority for social housing.

110884.explanation: Paying at the recycling center leads to more illegal waste dumping. Free disposal of private waste helps improve processing.

110885.explanation: The choice for solar panels lies with the landowner and must fit into the landscape. Citizen Interests does not support solar fields on agricultural land.

110888.explanation: A coffee shop attracts visitors and negatively affects the feeling of safety in the villages.

110891.explanation: In our municipality, room rentals are almost exclusively used for migrant workers. Banning single rooms in favor of small apartments (one- or two-room dwellings) ensures better quality and better housing.

110892.explanation: The center of Bodegraven has a parking problem. The number of parking spaces must not decrease, but the walking distance to home can be critically reviewed. A parking spot right at the door is not a given in the center.

110893.explanation: Bodegraven is a shopping village; car-free zones would make access to shops more difficult.

110895.explanation: The Bodegraven arc is a major infrastructure project. The national and provincial governments must take the lead; the municipality can co-invest in safe bicycle crossings.

110896.explanation: Housing for migrant workers should be limited to a maximum of 100 persons per location, as applied in the upcoming pilot.

110839.explanation: Our residents elect a municipal council from which they may expect well-considered decisions in the general interest over the next four years. It is important that this happens after a good debate and with a clear explanation.

110843.explanation: That can also be in the form of advice and guidance.

110844.explanation: We have enough of those. Diversity of businesses is desirable, and there are limits regarding the impact on the landscape qualities of the Green Heart.

110847.explanation: We see the housing of seasonal workers as a shared responsibility of employers and the municipality.

110855.explanation: That would lead to clutter in the landscape. Moreover, if farmers are allowed that, others in the countryside will probably want it as well...

110856.explanation: That is already made possible with the help of the Green Heart Pass.

110858.explanation: The municipality works methodically and in consultation with school boards to improve school buildings where and when necessary (with the Integral Housing Plan).

110860.explanation: Sports clubs themselves are responsible for keeping their financial affairs in order.

110863.explanation: Always well substantiated where, when, and why.

110864.explanation: That can also be done in Gouda as the regional central municipality.

110868.explanation: These homes are often not suitable for that and are located in places where parking and traffic problems can arise.

110870.explanation: That can very well be done through volunteer work.

110875.explanation: The mandatory road adjustments when changing to 30 km/h simply cost way too much.

110876.explanation: Freedom of choice regarding a care provider is a great good, just like the choice of a suitable school. However, the price of this care must be reasonable and market-conform.

110877.explanation: See also the answer to the previous question.

110878.explanation: Unfortunately that is not possible yet. It is important to continue paying off the municipality’s debts. This will eventually create room to lower the property tax.

110880.explanation: The tourist tax is already quite high. This is the favorite proposal of lazy politicians.

110881.explanation: That is certainly necessary to give our local companies the chance to develop further, retain employment, do more on circularity, and compensate the transformation of old industrial sites to housing.

110882.explanation: We wish everyone a common day of rest.

110883.explanation: We want temporary housing for status holders to be included in order to reduce pressure on regular housing.

110884.explanation: That seems especially undesirable for larger quantities.

110885.explanation: That is already the very last option. It could be different if the farmer agrees and if the solar panels can be spatially well integrated.

110888.explanation: The establishment of a coffee shop is a very bad idea, among other reasons from the perspective of safety and public health.

110891.explanation: That cannot be done arbitrarily and there is also a certain demand for rooms. Of course, the rules must be observed (and continuously enforced).

110892.explanation: There should be more parking spaces in the center of Bodegraven. To start with, the construction of a parking deck at the NS station, where all studies find it most urgently needed. This is also somewhat affordable.

110893.explanation: Then no shops would be left.

110895.explanation: Given the importance of this solution, it is justifiable, although most of the money should come from the national and provincial governments.

110839.explanation: As residents, we govern our own municipality. When a topic is very important, a referendum can be a nice and fair way to make a decision. This engages people in local democracy. It can also be done in other ways, such as setting up a youth council and discussion tables.

110843.explanation: This is the ideal picture: residents jointly produce their own green and affordable electricity with a wind turbine or solar panels. They use the electricity onsite, so the grid is not burdened. Energy cooperatives are an asset to our municipality and should be encouraged.

110844.explanation: Many large distribution centers have already been built in the Netherlands in recent years. They have a negative effect on the landscape and much of the work is done by poorly paid migrant workers. As far as we are concerned, we must stop the arrival of even more oversized distribution centers.

110847.explanation: Those who bring people to our municipality to work must, in consultation with the municipality, ensure that those workers have decent housing. This must comply with national housing standards. There is no place for exploitation in our municipality.

110855.explanation: There is a huge housing shortage and an extra home on the property can be a good solution. As an additional income source or as a suitable place for a parent who can no longer live independently. We want to make this possible for farmers who want it.

110856.explanation: Sports give a lot of energy and satisfaction. And it keeps you healthy. But for people on social assistance, sports are often too expensive. We want to maintain the reimbursement on the Green Heart Pass.

110858.explanation: A contribution must be made where necessary. This has already been provided for in the IHP.

110860.explanation: The municipality cares about the association life, but we must provide support where needed. This does not necessarily have to be financial support.

110862.explanation: BOAs (Special Enforcement Officers) and supervisors must also be more visible.

110863.explanation: Feeling safe on the street is very important. But more cameras are not the right solution in our opinion. Your privacy is a great asset that should not be violated lightly. Therefore, we prefer more street enforcement officers who can intervene immediately if something goes wrong.

110864.explanation: People must have access to a shelter for domestic violence victims. In Gouda, there is such a shelter for our municipality, and this has already been arranged.

110865.explanation: This is already the situation in our municipality. We want to maintain this.

110868.explanation: Our rural area is too vulnerable to this transition.

110870.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don't want to work. They need a stable base, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. We support guiding people towards contributing to society but do not make this a requirement.

110875.explanation: The built-up area is a residential area. It must be safe, but also healthy and clean. Lower speed results in less emission of harmful substances.

110876.explanation: Professional care providers deliver tailor-made care regardless of faith, culture, or belief. We are glad this can happen in our villages.

110877.explanation: Why focus only on the price? The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are huge. You cannot solve this by cutting more budgets and always choosing the cheapest option. We must primarily ensure certainty and provide quality help that addresses the concerns of our youth.

110878.explanation: The increase may be at most the inflation correction.

110880.explanation: As long as it reasonably corresponds to the tourist tax in the surrounding area.

110881.explanation: Two large business parks are on the agenda, and further expansion is undesirable.

110882.explanation: We find it important that shops can be open on Sundays for retailers and customers who want that. It is not an obligation. This also makes it much easier for single persons who work during the week to do important shopping.

110883.explanation: This group should be able to obtain an urgency statement together with other eligible persons. This is different from priority. This is also how it is currently arranged.

110884.explanation: Partly this is now free. But if you have a lot of bulky waste, you have to pay for it.

110885.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

110888.explanation: We aim for a healthy lifestyle, so we are against smoking or the use of mind-altering substances. The smoking ban must also be enforced. A coffee shop does not fit here.

110891.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage, and many people in our municipality live more spaciously than necessary. That is why we want to make it easier to rent out individual rooms, provided they are well-maintained and rented for a fair price.

110892.explanation: The center must be used more as a residential area. People who need to can obtain an exemption.

110893.explanation: The center must be used more as a residential area.

110895.explanation: There is no such thing as a free provision. There must always be a discussion about the amount.

110896.explanation: We have allowed this for one location because a problem had to be solved. We support small-scale locations.

110839.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal is very much in favor of participation. Speakers must certainly be listened to, and their input must be taken into account in decision-making. However, a referendum cannot replace the function of an elected representative body.

110843.explanation: According to Lokaal Liberaal, generating sustainable energy should basically be profitable on its own. Furthermore, the municipality's current financial capacity is too limited for this support. Support can, of course, be provided in ways other than financial.

110844.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal always encourages entrepreneurship. From a new distribution center in this municipality, we do expect it to contribute to local employment.

110847.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal believes that the municipality can also play a role in this.

110855.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal believes farmers should be free to use their land as they wish. Proper rules must be established for this.

110856.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal only wants to pay the sports club membership for children of people on welfare because sport is important for children's development. For adults, it is different.

110858.explanation: According to Lokaal Liberaal, this depends on the condition of all school buildings. They must meet legal standards. The current schedule for new school buildings can be followed.

110860.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal does not want to automatically provide financial support when a sports association is financially struggling. This must be assessed per sports association.

110862.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal wants BOAs to have sufficient authority to ensure safety. In addition, BOAs must also be deployed during evening hours.

110863.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal wants more targeted camera surveillance to prevent unsafe situations and theft.

110864.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal would like a shelter where victims of domestic violence can find a safe place.

110865.explanation: Providing a starter loan to people buying a house for the first time is not seen by Lokaal Liberaal as a core task of the municipality. Lokaal Liberaal does focus on building affordable starter homes.

110868.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal believes the municipality should allow vacation homes to be used as permanent residences, under the correct spatial and environmental conditions.

110870.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal supports that someone on welfare contributes to society by performing socially useful work. However, this should not hinder finding paid employment.

110875.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal does not see the need to limit the maximum speed to 30 km/h on all roads within built-up areas. Roads without separate bike paths do qualify for this.

110876.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal recognizes the desire to choose a care provider who matches faith, culture, or belief. However, it is up to the care provider to see if they can meet that wish. Lokaal Liberaal sees no role for the municipality in this.

110877.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal thinks quality relative to the price of youth care must be considered. So not just the price is relevant.

110878.explanation: Given the municipality's financial situation and Lokaal Liberaal's vision on recovery, a reduction of the property tax (ozb) is not possible.

110880.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal believes the tourist tax for holiday parks should be increased. It is indefensible that holiday parks currently fall under the reduced rate.

110881.explanation: To stimulate the economy, Lokaal Liberaal believes more business parks should be added in the municipality. This gives entrepreneurs the space to (continue to) establish themselves in the municipality, which benefits employment.

110882.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal believes that entrepreneurs who want to be open on Sundays should be allowed to do so.

110883.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal wants to give priority for social rental homes to people who already live or work in the municipality.

110884.explanation: Waste processing is becoming increasingly expensive. Lokaal Liberaal wants to keep the waste disposal charges lower for everyone by asking for a contribution.

110885.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal wants to encourage the installation of solar panels on roofs. However, owners of agricultural land should also have the option to use their land for solar panels.

110888.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal does not want to encourage the establishment of a coffeeshop. However, if an entrepreneur wants to start a coffeeshop, Lokaal Liberaal is not opposed, provided there is no nuisance.

110891.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal believes homeowners should be free to rent out rooms within the existing Dutch legislation.

110892.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal notes there is already a shortage of parking spaces in the center and therefore does not want to remove any parking spaces there.

110893.explanation: It is important for entrepreneurs that the center remains accessible by car. However, Lokaal Liberaal wants to explore the possibility of restricting access for cars on certain streets.

110895.explanation: The municipality should only invest money in the Bodegravenboog if it fits within the (limited) financial capabilities of the municipality.

110896.explanation: Lokaal Liberaal focuses on small groups (maximum 100 labor migrants) at one location. Local research has identified this as the most realistic option. Lokaal Liberaal is therefore not in favor of housing in large 'hotels'.

110839.explanation: We find resident participation important. We want to involve them as much as possible in decision-making, which is why we believe the participation regulation should be adjusted. Balancing interests remains a task for the council.

110843.explanation: We support initiatives from residents. We encourage involvement and civic responsibility.

110844.explanation: This does not fit the image of Bodegraven-Reeuwijk. We prefer to invest in local entrepreneurs and jobs for residents.

110847.explanation: Protection of labor migrants is desired. We want to give employers more responsibility. Employers must comply with strict national and municipal rules such as the Good Landlordship Act and the Building Decree.

110855.explanation: We favor infill development within existing built areas. We want to prevent building in rural areas.

110856.explanation: Fun is paramount in sports. That is why we choose an accessible sports environment. Sports should be available to everyone. This is currently arranged through the Groene Hart Pass.

110858.explanation: The current plans are good. No extra money needs to be spent on all school buildings. Some school buildings have already been improved in recent years and are in good condition. There are good plans for the other buildings. We aim for sustainable, quality school buildings.

110860.explanation: Clubs can raise their membership fees. It is important to maintain a diverse offering. It is not the municipality's responsibility to ensure a club's financial health.

110862.explanation: Boe is responsible for supervision and enforcement of livability in residential areas. More police capacity should be made available for safety.

110863.explanation: Following the CU motion (2024), mobile camera surveillance is in place. Expansion is desired. It is currently a pilot. We want to deploy this selectively where needed.

110864.explanation: We believe Bodegraven-Reeuwijk is not the right place for a women's shelter. We cooperate with the region. For example, there is a women's shelter in Gouda. We oppose violence against women and want to combat it.

110865.explanation: Based on Christian-social values of solidarity and mercy, we want to commit ourselves in Bodegraven-Reeuwijk to building affordable owner-occupied and rental homes for starters, others with limited budgets, and people with care needs.

110868.explanation: This discourages tourism and puts extra pressure on our vulnerable lake area.

110870.explanation: We are against the obligation. It will mainly cost paid civil servants to comply with and enforce this duty. FermWerk is fully committed to providing everyone with a suitable place in society. We support an appropriate place for everyone.

110875.explanation: We strive to limit the maximum speed within built-up areas to 30 km/h in as many places as possible and even 15 km/h in residential areas for the safety of children in particular. Only roads with public transport and connecting roads have a 50 km/h limit.

110876.explanation: Freedom of choice and respect for beliefs are important. Patients must feel at home and seen in the care that suits them.

110877.explanation: It must be about quality. We want care close to home, fewer rules, and more trust. We want to focus more on prevention. Price is a factor; we must strive for the best price-quality ratio.

110878.explanation: We want this tax to at least be frozen. Nationally, this tax is high in Bodegraven-Reeuwijk.

110880.explanation: This has increased significantly over the years, especially this year with the increased VAT.

110881.explanation: At present, the area is sufficiently expanded. Further expansion would lead to more residents and traffic.

110882.explanation: There are enough Sundays for shopping. We do not sense a need for more shopping Sundays. Shopkeepers should not be forced to open their stores on Sundays. The Christian Union is not in favor of expanding Sunday opening hours for shops.

110883.explanation: There are already rules for this. We have a legal duty to house them and sometimes to grant urgency. We will maintain this but will not expand it.

110884.explanation: For most waste, this is already free. Costs should be borne by the polluter, not the municipality.

110885.explanation: Place solar panels as much as possible on unused land near highways.

110888.explanation: Drug use will no longer be normalized. We want to do more against the increasing use and acceptance of drugs. Alcohol, drugs, and gambling destroy many lives. Addiction must not lead to the loss of human dignity.

110891.explanation: Provides the possibility for additional housing places.

110892.explanation: We support a safe area in the center for shoppers and cyclists. We want more and better parking spaces at the edge of the center. The center must remain accessible for the disabled, with car access where necessary.

110893.explanation: We want a quiet and safe city center where people can stroll and shop peacefully. Accessibility for disabled people remains important; for them, an exception to the car-free policy will apply.

110895.explanation: We will only support this if the Province and the national government cooperate with the plans. It is not the sole responsibility of the municipality of Bodegraven-Reeuwijk.

110896.explanation: We support small-scale care. We aim to provide care at the current locations. When more is needed, other locations must be sought. We advocate research into possibilities for collective living arrangements where young people and migrants can live together.

110839.explanation: Residents deserve real influence. A referendum can serve as an emergency brake for major decisions, alongside good participation, clear information, and a careful process.

110843.explanation: Local energy initiatives accelerate the transition and make it fairer. We assist with start-up funds, knowledge, and removing barriers, so more residents can participate.

110844.explanation: Large box buildings do not suit our villages. They cause extra freight traffic and pressure on the landscape. We opt for smart, small-scale business activities that add local value.

110847.explanation: Those who bring in people from outside must also ensure good housing. This can be on private grounds, provided it is safe, humane, well monitored, and fits the environment.

110855.explanation: This can offer perspective and help with succession. We make it possible where it fits the landscape, with quality requirements, and if it contributes to the housing task.

110856.explanation: Sport is about participation and staying healthy. We firmly maintain schemes like the Groene Hart Pass and expand where needed, so money is no barrier to sports.

110858.explanation: Good school buildings are a prerequisite for good education. We continue investing, as in recent years, in healthy, sustainable, and safe schools, with a realistic schedule per area.

110860.explanation: Sports clubs are the glue of our communities. We offer smart and targeted help with temporary problems, linked to sustainability, good governance, and future-proof facilities.

110862.explanation: Visible enforcement increases safety and livability. We deploy extra enforcement officers where needed, paying attention to prevention and the lake area.

110863.explanation: Privacy is a great value. Cameras are not a standard solution. We prefer human contact on the street, such as extra enforcement officers or neighborhood coaches. We install targeted temporary cameras only at proven hotspots.

110864.explanation: Those who feel unsafe at home must receive immediate help. We ensure sufficient safe shelter, preferably well organized regionally, with quick throughput and strong aftercare.

110865.explanation: In a tight market, this drives prices up and mainly helps those already close to buying. We choose more affordable construction, movement within the housing market, and fair allocation.

110868.explanation: We understand the need to solve housing shortages, but permanent residence can hinder recreation and causes issues with facilities and safety. We opt for tailor-made and genuine housing solutions.

110870.explanation: Trust works better than coercion. We offer guidance toward work or meaningful daily activities that suit someone’s situation. We encourage participation, but do not make it compulsorily standard.

110875.explanation: Safety first: 30 km/h where necessary, 50 km/h where possible. We decide based on layout and proven effect, combined with safe cycling routes and enforcement.

110876.explanation: We respect religion and culture and take wishes seriously. At the same time, care must be mainly appropriate and available: you choose as much as possible, but quality, accessibility, and waiting times always weigh in.

110877.explanation: Those who focus only on price lose quality and continuity. We choose proven effective assistance, good cooperation, and manage by results, not by the cheapest bid.

110878.explanation: We keep the property tax stable with inflation correction and make sharp budget choices. This keeps facilities in order without unexpected gaps or dismantling policies.

110880.explanation: We are already more expensive than neighboring municipalities. This harms recreational entrepreneurs and makes us less attractive than neighboring municipalities. We prefer to invest in quality of recreation and the lake area without additional levies.

110881.explanation: We remain an entrepreneurial municipality. Developments like Rijnhoek 2 are possible if sustainable, well accessible, and with room for local entrepreneurs and work close to home.

110882.explanation: Entrepreneurs know what works. We keep room for tailor-made solutions per area and sector: where there is a need, we make it possible, considering employees and livability. We do not enforce it.

110883.explanation: Housing is a basic right for everyone. We do not opt for automatic priority for one specific group, but for a fair distribution based on need. The real solution is significant construction of social housing for all those seeking accommodation.

110884.explanation: Disposal is already largely free and we keep it that way. A barrier-free recycling center reduces litter and encourages reuse.

110885.explanation: The energy transition is necessary. We do not exclude agricultural land outright, but steer towards dual functions and good landscape integration.

110888.explanation: By regulating it through a legal coffee shop, you remove trade from the streets and make agreements about safety and nuisance. Preferably transparent and manageable rather than tolerating without control.

110891.explanation: Room rental can help, provided it is safe and livable. Banning it pushes it underground. We manage permits, supervision, and limits per neighborhood to prevent nuisance.

110892.explanation: In the center, parking standards can be lowered. Short-term parking remains possible, and we strengthen parking on the outskirts with good walking routes and space for bicycles and greenery.

110893.explanation: We aim for car-free in the core shopping area, with loading and unloading time windows and exemptions. This creates space for pedestrians, terraces, and safety while retaining destination traffic where and when necessary.

110895.explanation: We are currently addressing bottlenecks and continue to vigorously lobby the Province and National Government for the Bodegravenbypass. This way, we tackle accessibility and livability simultaneously.

110896.explanation: No mega-complexes that overload the neighborhood. We opt for small-scale, well-regulated housing that fits the environment, with strict control and agreements with entrepreneurs.

statements.title: Bodegraven should ban housing migrant workers and seasonal workers in large "hotels."

titleParts.glossary: People from another country who work temporarily in the Netherlands.
People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Bodegraven-Reeuwijk should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling center should be completely free.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: See distribution center.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
Residents of Bodegraven-Reeuwijk should be able to express their opinion on important issues through a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Bodegraven-Reeuwijk moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Bodegraven-Reeuwijk should install more CCTV/camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: The municipality should allow more Sunday shopping days.
Bodegraven-Reeuwijk should establish a shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The center of Bodegraven should become car-free.
Bodegraven-Reeuwijk should develop more business parks.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
There should be fewer parking spaces in the center of Bodegraven.
Bodegraven should invest its own money in improving the connection between the A12 and the N11 (Bodegravenboog).
